channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal courts will not refer allegations that Supreme Court Justice Clarence Thomas may have violated ethics laws to the Justice Department, the judiciary's policymaking body said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker race, Trump's second term, Carter funeral: January brings flurry of political events
item.description: New Year's Day ushers in a month of political events and milestones the likes of which Washington hasn't seen in a while.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, NewsNation

item.title: New Orleans attacker had 'remote detonator' for explosives in French Quarter, Biden says
item.description: President Biden says the New Orleans attacker acted alone, and had a remote detonator for explosives before driving a pickup truck into a crowd of people, killing 14 and injuring 35 on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden gets the priciest gift from a foreign leader in 2023
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamo
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Kash on Demand
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear laughs off unflattering social media comments in a video with his wife
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear laughed off some unflattering comments aimed at him on social media in the past year, and had his teenage son translate what one barb meant, in a light-hearted video that shows his willingness to occasionally poke fun at himself.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FLASHBACK: Biden downplays ISIS threat to US, repeatedly says White supremacy 'most lethal' danger
item.description: President Biden repeatedly said White supremacy posed the greatest threat to the U.S., explicitly saying terrorist organizations such as ISIS could not compare.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill would exempt Tennessee SROs from immigration status reporting requirement
item.description: A new bill sponsored by a Republican senator would shield school resource officers from having to report a student's immigration status to the federal government in hopes of protecting children in schools.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Hochul's Christmastime boast of safer subway came amid string of alarming violent attacks
item.description: New York Democratic Gov. Kathy Hochul celebrated a statistical decrease in New York City subway crime, including minor and major offenses, just days before several fatal and near-fatal incidents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Progressives face an existential crisis under Trump
item.description: Progressives in Washington are facing an existential crisis like never before as they brace for the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to headline 'victory rally' in nation's capital on eve of inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to hold what's being called a “victory rally" in Washington D.C. the day before he's sworn in to a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With a nod to her mentor Shirley Chisholm, Rep. Barbara Lee exits Congress as a renegade herself
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Barbara Lee has always stood apart, a matter-of-fact renegade with a long list of firsts. In high school, she was the first Black student to integrate her Southern California cheerleading squad.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio updates election forms to support transgender candidates
item.description: The change followed the disqualification of a transgender woman who failed to list legal names used in the past five years on a petition.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he is planning to attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Tuesday that he's planning to attend the funeral of former President Jimmy Carter. Asked about it as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump responded, "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls it the 'center of the universe.' Mar-a-Lago is a magnet for those seeking influence
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The cars begin lining up early in the morning to be screened by Secret Service agents under white tents near the fence that surrounds President-elect Donald Trump’s vast south Florida estate.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: California regulation requires insurance companies to offer coverage in high-risk areas
item.description: California insurers will be required to sell coverage in wildfire-prone regions that have seen an insurer exodus in recent years, state Insurance Commissioner Ricardo Lara announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker math: Breaking down how many votes Johnson needs to keep his gavel
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) heads to the House floor on Friday in hopes of being formally reelected Speaker. While Johnson has the endorsement of President-elect Trump to remain Speaker, one Republican is pledging to oppose him, and several others are not committing to supporting Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Texas driver license offices reopen after systemwide outage
item.description: The outage appeared only to affect the Texas public safety department. Driver license offices reopened after the New Year’s Day holiday.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Stephen Simpson, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. Blumenthal applauds law that expands Social Security benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (WTNH) -- Nearly 3 million Americans could see a boost in Social Security payments, thanks to a bipartisan act awaiting President Joe Biden's signature.
item.dc:creator: Eva Zymaris, NewsNation

item.title: Biden announces nearly $2.5 billion more in military aid for Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that the United States will send nearly $2.5 billion more in weapons to Ukraine as his administration works quickly to spend all the money it has available to help Kyiv fight off Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump gives complete and total endorsement of Johnson as Speaker
item.description: President-elect Trump offered his complete and total endorsement of Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Monday, days before a House vote on Friday to elect a new Speaker. "Speaker Mike Johnson is a good, hard working, religious man. He will do the right thing, and we will continue to WIN.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5
